title: echinacea pallida var. angustifolia
intro: Echinacea pallida var. angustifolia, also known as narrow-leaved purple coneflower, is a perennial plant species within the Echinacea genus. It is a subspecies of Echinacea pallida and is commonly found in the prairies and dry meadows of the Great Plains region in North America. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are believed to enhance the immune system and provide relief from colds and flu. Echinacea pallida var. angustifolia is often prepared as a dried root or in capsule form, and it can also be consumed as a tea or tincture, which are said to provide various health benefits, including reducing the severity and duration of colds, improving respiratory health, and boosting the body's natural defense against infections.
